import json
import pandas as pd
import re
from collections import Counter

file_path = locals()['var_functions.query_db:12']
with open(file_path, 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

business_categories = {}
business_ids = []

category_mappings = {
    'education': ['education', 'elementary schools', 'preschools', 'montessori schools', 'child care', 'day care', 'school'],
    'beauty & spas': ['beauty & spas', 'hair salons', 'skin care', 'makeup artists', 'nail salons', 'waxing', 'cosmetics', 'tanning', 'teeth whitening', 'cosmetic dentists', 'dentists', 'hair removal', 'hair stylists'],
    'active life': ['active life', 'gun/rifle ranges'],
    'shopping': ['shopping', 'antiques', 'lighting fixtures', 'flowers & gifts', 'florists'],
    'restaurants': ['restaurants', 'chinese', 'blow dry/out services'],
    'health & medical': ['health & medical', 'medical centers', 'internal medicine', 'doctors'],
    'local services': ['local services'],
    'event planning': ['event planning', 'wedding planning'],
    'automotive': ['automotive', 'gas stations', 'taxis', 'transportation'],
    'food': ['food', 'candy stores', 'specialty food', 'coffee & tea'],
    'home services': ['home services'],
    'financial services': ['financial services'],
    'hotels & travel': ['hotels & travel']
}

def categorize_business(description):
    desc_lower = description.lower()
    
    if 'services in' in desc_lower:
        services_part = desc_lower.split('services in', 1)[1]
    elif 'services including' in desc_lower:
        services_part = desc_lower.split('services including', 1)[1]
    elif 'category of' in desc_lower:
        return ['restaurants']
    else:
        return ['other']
    
    found_categories = []
    for category, keywords in category_mappings.items():
        for keyword in keywords:
            if keyword in services_part:
                found_categories.append(category)
                break
    
    return found_categories if found_categories else ['other']

for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']

    'beauty & spas': ['beauty & spas', 'hair salons', 'skin care', 'makeup artists', 'nail salons', 'waxing', 'cosmetics', 'tanning', 'teeth whitening', 'cosmetic dentists', 'dentists', 'hair removal', 'hair stylists'],
    'active life': ['active life', 'gun/rifle ranges'],
    'shopping': ['shopping', 'antiques', 'lighting fixtures', 'flowers & gifts', 'florists'],
    'restaurants': ['restaurants', 'chinese', 'blow dry/out services'],
    'health & medical': ['health & medical', 'medical centers', 'internal medicine', 'doctors'],
    'local services': ['local services'],
    'event planning': ['event planning', 'wedding planning'],
    'automotive': ['automotive', 'gas stations', 'taxis', 'transportation'],
    'food': ['food', 'candy stores', 'specialty food', 'coffee & tea'],
    'home services': ['home services'],
    'financial services': ['financial services'],
    'hotels & travel': ['hotels & travel']
}

def categorize_business(description):
    desc_lower = description.lower()
    
    if 'services in' in desc_lower:
        services_part = desc_lower.split('services in', 1)[1]
    elif 'services including' in desc_lower:
        services_part = desc_lower.split('services including', 1)[1]
    elif 'category of' in desc_lower:
        return ['restaurants']
    else:
        return ['other']
    
    found_categories = []
    for category, keywords in category_mappings.items():
        for keyword in keywords:
            if keyword in services_part:
                found_categories.append(category)
                break
    
    return found_categories if found_categories else ['other']

for business in businesses:
    business_id = business['business_id']
    description = business['description']
    
    categories = categorize_business(description)
    business_categories[business_id] = categories
    business_ids.append(business_id)

category_counts = Counter()
for categories in business_categories.values():
    for category in categories:
        category_counts[category] += 1

sorted_categories = sorted(category_counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

data_to_save = {
    'business_categories': business_categories,
    'category_counts': dict(category_counts),
    'sorted_categories': sorted_categories,
    'total_businesses': len(businesses)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(data_to_save))

args.code:
import json

beauty_spa_ratings = {
    'businessref_33': {'rating': 3.5217391304347827, 'count': 23},
    'businessref_64': {'rating': 3.7142857142857144, 'count': 7},
    'businessref_68': {'rating': 1.7619047619047619, 'count': 21},
    'businessref_11': {'rating': 4.2, 'count': 10},
    'businessref_38': {'rating': 3.1176470588235294, 'count': 17},
    'businessref_81': {'rating': 3.6666666666666665, 'count': 6}
}

total_reviews = sum(data['count'] for data in beauty_spa_ratings.values())
weighted_sum = sum(data['rating'] * data['count'] for data in beauty_spa_ratings.values())
average_rating = weighted_sum / total_reviews

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'category': 'Beauty & Spas',
    'business_count': len(beauty_spa_ratings),
    'average_rating': round(average_rating, 2),
    'total_reviews': total_reviews
}))

var_functions.query_db:42.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

val_args.code:
import json

beauty_spa_ratings = {
    'businessref_33': {'rating': 3.5217391304347827, 'count': 23},
    'businessref_64': {'rating': 3.7142857142857144, 'count': 7},
    'businessref_68': {'rating': 1.7619047619047619, 'count': 21},
    'businessref_11': {'rating': 4.2, 'count': 10},
    'businessref_38': {'rating': 3.1176470588235294, 'count': 17},
    'businessref_81': {'rating': 3.6666666666666665, 'count': 6}
}

total_reviews = sum(data['count'] for data in beauty_spa_ratings.values())
weighted_sum = sum(data['rating'] * data['count'] for data in beauty_spa_ratings.values())
average_rating = weighted_sum / total_reviews

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    'category': 'Beauty & Spas',
    'business_count': len(beauty_spa_ratings),
    'average_rating': round(average_rating, 2),
    'total_reviews': total_reviews
}))
